cards.back: Change made to data is only written when the original data is modified

cards.front: What is the primary purpose of Copy-on-write (CoW) in operating systems?
cards.back: To reduce the number of writes to disk and improve performance
When data is modified frequently or in a database

cards.front: What are the benefits of using Copy-on-write (CoW) in operating systems?
cards.back: Reduced disk I/O, improved performance, and reduced risk of data corruption

cards.front: What is an alternative to Copy-on-write (CoW)?
cards.back: Journaling, which logs all changes to disk before making them permanent
